text.title: codemod-recipe-library Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the codemod-recipe-library methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Use a typed AST (jscodeshift / ts-morph / libcst / ast-grep) — never regex over source. Regex misses comments, multi-line, string-literal collisions.
Every recipe ships with a before fixture and an after fixture, byte-equal to the expected transform output.
rule.rationale: Without paired fixtures, recipes silently rot.

rule.statement: A CI smoke test runs every recipe on every PR — applies the recipe to the fixture, diffs against the expected after.
Recipes are named after the transform action (verb-noun: rename-component-prop, replace-render-with-createRoot). Names mirror invocation.
rule.rationale: Consistent verb-noun names map directly to CLI invocations.

rule.statement: Recipes carry version + framework_target metadata; stale recipes (> 1 framework minor) flagged on read.
Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
